title: PF Ethnographer Settings
description: Participant-supplied configuration for the pf-ethnographer skill. Stored at $OPENCLAW_DATA_DIR/skills/pf-ethnographer/settings.json

schema_version.description: Settings schema version. Do not modify.

research_team_email.description: Email address of the research team recipient. Set by the participant. When configured, enables 'Approve & Send to Research Team' in pulse review. When null (default), only Copy/Export mode is available. Set via 'Configure research team recipient' command.

retention_days.description: Number of days to retain events and report files. After each pulse, events and report directories older than this value are automatically deleted. Participant may increase up to 90 days.

always_review_before_send.description: Must always be true. Sanitized reports must be reviewed and explicitly approved by the participant before any sharing. This setting is non-overridable — the value cannot be set to false.

pulse_timezone.description: Timezone used for pulse scheduling. Non-overridable.

pulse_times_local.description: Scheduled pulse times in pulse_timezone. Non-overridable.

over_redact.description: When true, instructs the Sanitizer to use aggressive heuristic redaction, including third-party person names and uncertain patterns. Strongly recommended to keep true. Set to false only if you are frequently seeing false-positive redactions that damage report readability.

log_general_usage.description: When true (default), log all OpenClaw usage events regardless of personal-finance relevance. When false, only events with pf_relevance_score >= min_pf_relevance_for_index are logged. Setting to false reduces data volume but narrows the behavioral context available for research.

min_pf_relevance_for_index.description: Events with pf_relevance_score >= this value are added to finance_index.json for fast retrieval and are featured prominently in pulse reports. Events below this threshold appear in the General Usage section.

consent_version.description: Version string of the consent notice the participant agreed to. Used to detect when consent must be re-presented after a notice update.

consent_timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the participant last gave consent. Set automatically at consent time.

no_events_silent.description: When true (default), pulses with no new events are recorded silently without interrupting the participant. When false, the participant receives a brief 'No new events' notification at pulse time.

export_format.description: Default format for exported pulse packages. 'markdown' exports .md files only. 'json' exports the raw event list (sanitized). 'both' exports both.

weekly_digest_day.description: Day of the week on which the 17:00 pulse also compiles and presents a weekly digest automatically, in addition to the regular pulse.
